skill.name: maxhub-linkedin
skill.description: LinkedIn professional data query assistant. Covers user profiles, company information, job search, posts, comments, ads with V1/V2 API support. Requires MAXHUB_API_KEY configured in the environment.
skill.summary: AI-powered LinkedIn data assistant for professional profiles, company research, job search, and content analysis via the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate LinkedIn API client using the MaxHub API. Of the 855 static findings, the vast majority are false positives from markdown documentation files misparsed by the static analyzer (reference docs, README examples). The real concern is in SKILL.md where the AI is instructed to execute curl commands with the MAXHUB_API_KEY as a Bearer token - this is inherent to an API client skill but carries risk of credential exposure and potential command injection. The skill is safe to publish with a MEDIUM risk rating and documented warnings.

medium_findings.title: AI-instructed curl execution with API credentials
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the AI assistant to construct and execute curl commands using the MAXHUB_API_KEY as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Commands include GET and POST requests with user-supplied endpoint parameters. This is expected behavior for an API client but carries real risk of credential exposure via shell history, process listing, or command injection if user input is not sanitized before being inserted into URL parameters.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access for API authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill reads MAXHUB_API_KEY from the environment and uses it directly in curl command Authorization headers. While this is a documented and necessary practice for API client skills, it represents a credential exposure risk if the AI assistant outputs the key value or if the command execution leaks the key through error messages or verbose output.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation files (false positive)
low_findings.description: All 23 network URL detections point to the legitimate MaxHub API endpoint (https://www.aconfig.cn) in markdown documentation. These include the API base URL, registration page, and setup instructions. No evidence of data exfiltration or malicious endpoints. FALSE POSITIVE - these are required for the API client to function.

low_findings.title: Shell backtick patterns in README and reference files (false positive)
low_findings.description: Over 600 detections of Ruby/shell backtick execution in README.md, README_CN.md, and all reference files. These are markdown documentation files containing curl command examples for educational purposes. The AI does not execute commands from reference files directly - it reads them for API endpoint documentation. Only the commands explicitly in SKILL.md are executed. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance in reference files (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected weak crypto algorithms and system reconnaissance patterns in reference markdown files. These detections are artifacts of parsing API documentation tables containing parameter names, example values, and endpoint paths. No actual cryptographic weakness or reconnaissance code exists - these are purely descriptive markdown API references. FALSE POSITIVE.

dangerous_patterns.title: Curl command construction with secret injection in Authorization header
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md defines a pattern where the AI constructs curl commands by embedding the MAXHUB_API_KEY directly into an Authorization: Bearer header. The pattern shown is: curl -s 'https://www.aconfig.cn/api/v1/linkedin/{endpoint}?{params}' -H 'Authorization: Bearer ${MAXHUB_API_KEY}'. This exposes the API key in the shell command string, making it visible in process listings, shell history, and error output.

content.user_title: Query LinkedIn professional data with AI-powered API assistance
content.value_statement: Accessing LinkedIn profiles, companies, jobs, and content requires time-consuming manual browsing. This skill connects to the MaxHub API so you can query structured professional data through natural language for research, recruitment, and business analysis.
actual_capabilities: Look up detailed LinkedIn user profiles including experience, education, skills, certifications, and publications
Retrieve company information such as employee count, affiliated pages, job listings, and stock data
Search for jobs, people, and posts across LinkedIn with filters for location, industry, and date
Access post details, comments, reactions, and reposts for content analysis
Query advertising data and ad details from LinkedIn campaigns
Support both English and Chinese language responses with appropriate number formatting
limitations: Requires a valid MAXHUB_API_KEY configured in the environment - cannot function without one
Data quality depends on the third-party MaxHub API and may not reflect real-time LinkedIn data
Some API endpoints may have usage limits or rate restrictions that block frequent queries
Does not support posting, messaging, writing comments, or any write operations on LinkedIn

use_cases.title: Business intelligence research
use_cases.description: Research target companies by retrieving their profile, employee count, recent job postings, and affiliated pages. Helps sales teams prepare for client meetings.

use_cases.title: Talent acquisition and recruitment
use_cases.description: Search for candidates by job title, location, and skills. Review candidate profiles with experience, education, and certification details. Analyze job market trends.

use_cases.title: Competitive monitoring and analysis
use_cases.description: Track competitor company changes, hiring patterns, and content activity. Generate reports on industry trends by comparing multiple companies side by side.

prompt_templates.title: Look up a LinkedIn user profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the LinkedIn user profile for {username}. Include their headline, location, experience, skills, and education.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researching a professional contact or candidate

prompt_templates.title: Research a company in detail
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the company {company_name}. Get their profile, employee data, recent job listings, and affiliated pages. Summarize the key findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing for a client meeting or sales call

prompt_templates.title: Search for jobs by criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for {job_title} jobs in {location} posted in the past {time_period}. Show me the top results with company names and application links.

prompt_templates.title: Full company competitive analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Do a full analysis of {company_a} and compare it with {company_b}. Get profiles, employee growth trends, job openings, and recent posts for both. Present a side-by-side comparison.
prompt_templates.scenario: Competitive intelligence analysis for strategy planning

output_examples.input: Look up the LinkedIn profile for user johndoe
output_examples.output:
Here is the profile summary for johndoe:

- Name: John Doe
- Headline: Senior Software Engineer at TechCorp
- Location: San Francisco Bay Area
- Experience: Senior Engineer at TechCorp (2020-present), Software Engineer at StartupX (2017-2020)
- Skills: Python, JavaScript, AWS, System Design, Kubernetes
- Education: BS Computer Science, Stanford University
- Certifications: AWS Solutions Architect

output_examples.input: Research the company TechCorp and summarize their recent activity
output_examples.output:
Here is the analysis for TechCorp:

- Industry: Enterprise Software
- Company Size: 1,001 to 5,000 employees
- Headquarters: San Francisco, CA
- Recent Job Openings: 12 positions including Senior Engineer, Product Manager, Data Scientist
- Affiliated Pages: TechCorp India, TechCorp Europe
- Key finding: Strong hiring growth in AI and machine learning roles suggests a strategic focus on AI products.

output_examples.input: Search for machine learning engineer jobs in New York posted this month
output_examples.output:
Found 24 machine learning engineer jobs in New York posted this month.

Top results:
1. ML Engineer at DataCorp - New York, NY (Easy Apply)
2. Senior Machine Learning Engineer at AI Labs - New York, NY
3. Machine Learning Engineer at FinTech Co - New York, NY (under 10 applicants)

Summary: Most roles require Python and TensorFlow. Average seniority level is mid to senior.

best_practices: Always specify the company name or LinkedIn username clearly for accurate query results
Use specific search criteria like location, date range, and job type to narrow down large result sets
Combine multiple queries in a single request for deep analysis (profile plus experience plus skills)
anti_patterns: Do not share or expose the MAXHUB_API_KEY value in any output or response under any circumstances
Avoid making excessive sequential queries when they can be run in parallel to respect rate limits
Do not request sensitive personal data or attempt to access LinkedIn features not exposed by the API

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you must register at aconfig.cn and configure the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable before using this skill.

faq.question: What types of LinkedIn data can this skill access?
faq.answer: It can access user profiles, company details, job listings, posts, comments, reactions, reposts, and advertising data.

faq.question: Is the data from this skill real-time?
faq.answer: Data comes from the MaxHub API and freshness depends on their caching policies. It may not reflect real-time LinkedIn data.

faq.question: Can this skill post or send messages on LinkedIn?
faq.answer: No, this skill is read-only. It cannot create posts, send messages, write comments, or perform any write operations.

faq.question: Does this skill support both English and Chinese?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill detects the user's language and responds accordingly with appropriate number formatting.

faq.question: Are there usage limits for the MaxHub API?
faq.answer: The MaxHub API may impose rate limits or usage quotas. Respect 429 rate limit responses and adjust query frequency.
